description: Golden evaluation dataset for MAS Compliance RAG pipeline

questions.query: What are MAS's requirements for customer due diligence on politically exposed persons under Notice 626?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Notice 626 requires banks to have risk management procedures to determine whether a customer or beneficial owner is a PEP. Enhanced CDD measures include establishing the source of wealth and source of funds, obtaining senior management approval for establishing or continuing business relationships, and conducting enhanced ongoing monitoring of PEP relationships.

questions.query: What governance structures does MAS expect for AI risk management in financial institutions?
questions.ground_truth_answer: MAS expects a governance framework covering model risk management with clear accountability, independent validation, and ongoing monitoring. The FEAT principles require fairness, ethics, accountability, and transparency. The AI Model Risk Management paper outlines good practices observed in banks for AI governance and oversight, model inventory, risk materiality assessment, and lifecycle governance covering development, validation, deployment, and monitoring. The MindForge Handbook operationalises these FEAT principles into practical implementation guidance.

questions.query: What are the key requirements in MAS TRM Guidelines for incident reporting?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The TRM Guidelines require FIs to establish an incident management framework to restore affected IT services quickly (Section 7.7). The framework should cover procedures for handling IT incidents including cyber incidents, evidence maintenance for investigation and diagnosis, and defined roles for recording, analysis, escalation, decision-making, resolution, and monitoring. Problem management (Section 7.8) covers root cause analysis and lessons learnt. The TRM Guidelines also separately require a cyber incident response and management plan (Section 12.3). Note: the one-hour MAS notification requirement is found in the BCM Guidelines (Section 9.6), not the TRM Guidelines.

questions.query: What are the key differences between the outsourcing requirements for banks versus non-bank financial institutions under MAS guidelines?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Both sets of guidelines cover the same core topics: board/senior management responsibilities, risk evaluation, due diligence of service providers, outsourcing agreements, sub-contracting, confidentiality and security, BCM, monitoring and control, audit and inspection, cross-border outsourcing, intra-group outsourcing, and outsourcing of internal audit. Both include Cloud Computing annexes with nearly identical content. The bank version operates within the Banking Act framework using 'MOORS' terminology and references MAS Notices 658/1121, while the non-bank version applies to FIs under the Financial Services and Markets Act 2022. The substantive risk management expectations are largely equivalent, with the bank version having somewhat more detailed sub-contracting provisions for MAS audit rights. Both take effect 11 December 2024.

questions.query: What are the five accountability outcomes under MAS's Individual Accountability and Conduct Guidelines?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The IAC Guidelines set five accountability and conduct outcomes: (1) Senior managers responsible for managing and conducting the FI's core functions are clearly identified; (2) Senior managers are fit and proper for their roles, and held responsible for the actions of their employees and the conduct of the business under their purview; (3) The FI's governance framework supports senior managers' performance of their roles and responsibilities, with a clear and transparent management structure and reporting relationships; (4) Material risk personnel are fit and proper for their roles, and subject to effective risk governance, and appropriate incentive structures and standards of conduct; (5) The FI has a framework that promotes and sustains among all employees the desired conduct.

questions.query: What does MAS require for business continuity management testing and exercises?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The BCM Guidelines require FIs to conduct regular and comprehensive testing (Section 7). Test objectives include validating BCP effectiveness, familiarising personnel with roles, practising crisis management decision-making under simulated conditions, stress testing BCPs under severe but plausible scenarios, and verifying that SRTOs and RTOs can be met. Test types can range from call-tree activation, crisis management exercises, business process recovery tests, and data restoration testing. The 2022 revision focuses on critical business services with Service Recovery Time Objectives (SRTOs) as a key metric.

questions.query: What are MAS's expectations for AML/CFT transaction monitoring controls?
questions.ground_truth_answer: MAS expects FIs to have strong governance over transaction monitoring (board/senior management oversight), know-your-customer foundations for TM, risk-based calibration of monitoring parameters, thresholds and scenarios (including back-testing and data integrity), robust implementation through pre-transaction checks and proper alert handling, and resolution processes including STR filing, quality assurance, and post-mortem reviews. The guidance also covers risk awareness through staff training and competence.

questions.query: How should a bank handle source of wealth verification during customer onboarding according to MAS supervisory expectations?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The October 2024 AML/CFT Supervisory Expectations paper covers customer risk assessment, identification of material red flags in CDD documents, source of wealth establishment, risk mitigation measures, and holistic monitoring of accounts. Guidelines to Notice 626 provide interpretive guidance on CDD including source of wealth and source of funds requirements. Banks should apply rigor in assessing the plausibility of customers' source of wealth, apply enhanced measures for higher-risk customers including independent corroboration, and document their assessments.

questions.query: What are MAS's requirements for AML/CFT name screening practices?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The information paper on name screening practices covers governance of name screening, setting screening parameters, alert resolution processes, and fuzzy matching capabilities. Published April 2022 based on thematic inspection findings, it addresses how FIs should configure name matching algorithms, manage screening alert volumes, and ensure effective sanctions and PEP screening.

questions.query: What is the COSMIC platform and what are its requirements for financial institutions?
questions.ground_truth_answer: COSMIC is an FI-to-FI information sharing platform established by Notice FSM-N02 (dated 28 March 2024). The full form of the acronym is not defined in the source documents. It enables prescribed financial institutions to share risk information about relevant parties (customers, former customers, and prospects) for AML/CFT purposes through Requests, Responses, Disclosures, and Listings. The Guidelines to MAS Notice FSM-N02 (18 October 2024) provide interpretive guidance on the platform's requirements including internal policies, risk assessment, information sharing obligations, platform access security, record keeping, and outsourcing requirements.

questions.query: What does MAS's enforcement monograph say about its enforcement approach and strategy?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The 2018 Enforcement Monograph outlines MAS's enforcement approach, powers, and strategy. It describes how MAS decides which cases to pursue, the range of enforcement tools available (civil penalties, criminal prosecution, prohibition orders, etc.), and the principles guiding enforcement decisions.

questions.query: What are MAS's Fair Dealing outcomes that financial institutions should deliver to customers?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The May 2024 revision of the Fair Dealing Guidelines sets out five fair dealing outcomes that boards and senior management are responsible for delivering to customers: (1) Customers have confidence that they deal with financial institutions where fair dealing is central to the corporate culture; (2) Financial institutions offer products and services that are suitable for their target customer segments; (3) Customers are served by competent representatives; (4) Customers receive clear, relevant, and timely information that accurately represent the products and services offered and delivered; (5) Financial institutions handle customer complaints in an independent, effective, and prompt manner.

questions.query: How do the FEAT principles relate to the MindForge AI Risk Management handbook?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The FEAT Principles (2018) established the foundational AI governance framework covering fairness, ethics, accountability, and transparency for financial institutions using AI and data analytics. The MindForge Handbook (January 2026) builds upon and extends the FEAT principles into comprehensive, practical implementation guidance for AI governance and risk management. Project MindForge was launched as a collaborative industry initiative in 2023, and this Handbook was formally launched at the Singapore Fintech Festival in November 2025.

questions.query: How many enforcement actions has MAS taken for AML/CFT violations?
questions.ground_truth_answer: MAS has taken 35 enforcement actions for AML/CFT violations (violation_category = 'aml_cft').

questions.query: What types of enforcement actions has MAS taken, and how many of each type?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Distribution by action_type from 337 enforcement_actions: prohibition_order (166), criminal_conviction (64), composition_penalty (50), civil_penalty (25), reprimand (14), other (11), licence_revocation (7).

questions.query: Which regulatory instruments are currently in force and applicable to the banking sector?
questions.ground_truth_answer: 21 regulatory instruments are currently in force and applicable to the banking sector, including: MAS Notice 626 and Guidelines to Notice 626, TRM Guidelines and Notice FSM-N21, Internal Controls Guidelines, IAC Guidelines and IAC FAQs, BCM Guidelines, Outsourcing Guidelines (Banks), Fair Dealing Guidelines, MAS Notice FSM-N02 (COSMIC) and Guidelines to FSM-N02, FEAT Principles, AI Model Risk Management Paper, MindForge Handbook, Enforcement Monograph, Enforcement Report 2023/2024, and AML/CFT information papers on supervisory expectations, private banking controls, transaction monitoring, and name screening.

questions.query: How many enforcement actions were taken against institutions versus individuals?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Of the 337 enforcement actions, 207 were taken against individuals and 130 against institutions.

questions.query: What enforcement actions has MAS taken related to Goldman Sachs?
questions.ground_truth_answer: 8 enforcement records match Goldman Sachs across 4 entities: Goldman Sachs (Asia) L.L.C. (prohibition_order 2018-12-19 fit_and_proper; other 2020-10-23 1MDB), Goldman Sachs Singapore Pte (prohibition_order 2018-12-19 fit_and_proper; other 2020-10-23 media reporting; other 2020-10-23 1MDB), Goldman Sachs International (criminal_conviction 2020-10-23 market_abuse), The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc (prohibition_order 2023-09-05 fit_and_proper; other 2020-10-23 media reporting).

questions.query: List all enforcement actions classified as criminal convictions for market abuse.
questions.ground_truth_answer: 28 criminal convictions for market abuse, including: five former remisiers charged for false trading (2020), Goldman Sachs International (stock market manipulation, 2020), Soh Chee Wen and Goh Hin Calm convicted as 2013 penny stocks crash masterminds (2022), Blumont/Asiasons/LionGold stock manipulation sentences (2022), multiple insider trading and false trading convictions through 2025.

questions.query: What MAS regulatory instruments cover AI governance topics?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Instruments with 'ai_governance' in topic_tags: FEAT Principles (2018), AI Model Risk Management Paper (2024), MindForge Handbook (2026), and AI Risk Management Consultation (2025).

questions.query: How many enforcement actions did MAS take in 2023?
questions.ground_truth_answer: MAS took 47 enforcement actions in 2023: 30 prohibition orders, 11 criminal convictions, 5 composition penalties, and 1 civil penalty.

questions.query: How do Singapore's AML/CFT requirements compare with Hong Kong's approach?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Cross-jurisdictional comparison requiring web search. The corpus contains only Singapore MAS documents. The answer should discuss differences in regulatory frameworks between MAS and HKMA/SFC approaches to AML/CFT.

questions.query: Has MAS issued any new regulatory guidance since April 2026?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The corpus was last updated 2026-04-05. Any publications after this date require web search. The agent should recognise this as a temporal gap query and use web search.

questions.query: What was the industry response to MAS's proposed AI Risk Management guidelines consultation?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The AI Risk Management consultation paper (November 2025) is in the corpus but external industry feedback and commentary would need web search. The agent may combine vector search for the consultation paper content with web search for industry responses.

questions.query: Are there any upcoming MAS consultation deadlines that financial institutions should be aware of?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Requires real-time information about open consultations. The corpus has historical consultation papers but not live deadline data. The agent should use web search.

questions.query: What recent regulatory changes has Singapore made to its payment services licensing framework?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The corpus has limited payment services content. Recent changes to the Payment Services Act or licensing framework would need web search for up-to-date information.

questions.query: What AML control weaknesses has MAS identified in enforcement actions, and what do the guidelines recommend to address them?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL provides enforcement action records for violation_category='aml_cft' showing institutions penalised and action types taken. Vector search provides AML/CFT guideline content from Notice 626, Guidelines to Notice 626, and supervisory expectations describing the expected control standards for CDD, transaction monitoring, and ongoing monitoring.
ground_truth_aspects: count or examples of AML enforcement actions

questions.query: How many enforcement actions relate to technology risk, and what does the TRM Guidelines say about the areas where breaches occurred?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL query for violation_category = 'technology_risk' returns 0 enforcement actions — there are no technology risk enforcement actions in the database. The actual violation categories are: fit_and_proper (163), other (84), market_abuse (44), aml_cft (35), business_conduct (9), disclosure (2). Vector search retrieves TRM Guidelines content on the relevant control areas including cyber security, incident reporting, and IT governance.
ground_truth_aspects: TRM guideline requirements for breached areas

questions.query: What is the total number of prohibition orders issued by MAS, and what conduct standards under the IAC Guidelines are these individuals expected to uphold?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL gives count from action_type = 'prohibition_order' (approximately 166). Vector search retrieves IAC Guidelines content on conduct standards, fitness and propriety requirements for senior managers and material risk personnel, and the five accountability and conduct outcomes: (1) clear identification of senior managers for core functions, (2) senior managers being fit and proper and held responsible for their employees' actions, (3) governance frameworks supporting senior managers with clear management structures, (4) material risk personnel being fit and proper and subject to effective risk governance and appropriate incentive structures, and (5) the FI having a framework to promote and sustain desired conduct among all employees.

questions.query: Which financial institutions received composition penalties for AML breaches in 2023 or later, and what are the key CDD requirements under Notice 626 they were found to have failed?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL for action_type = 'composition_penalty' AND violation_category = 'aml_cft' AND action_date >= '2023-01-01' returns 25 records across 3 enforcement events: (1) 2023-06-21 — DBS Bank, OCBC Singapore, Citibank N.A., Swiss Life, Citadelle (MAS penalises 3 banks and insurer), (2) 2024-05-07 — Swiss-Asia Financial Services and its CEO/COO (S$2.5M penalty), (3) 2025-07-04 — Citibank Singapore Limited, Citibank N.A., UBS AG, Credit Suisse, LGT Bank, Bank Julius Baer, Blue Ocean Invest, Trident Trust, and multiple individuals (9 FIs for AML breaches). Vector search retrieves Notice 626 CDD requirements these institutions were expected to comply with.

questions.query: Tell me about MAS enforcement actions related to the 1MDB case — which entities were involved and what regulations did they breach?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL finds 8 entries mentioning 1MDB in descriptions: (1) Four individuals with prohibition orders dated 2017-03-13 (Yak Yew Chee, Yvonne Seah Yew Foong, Tim Leissner, Jens Fred Sturzenegger — all fit_and_proper), (2) Four entries dated 2020-10-23 related to AGC/CAD/MAS action on Goldman Sachs 1MDB bond offerings (Tim Leissner, Goldman Sachs Singapore Pte, Ng Chong Hwa, Goldman Sachs (Asia) L.L.C.). Note: BSI Bank and Credit Suisse/UOB do not appear in 1MDB description search results. Vector search retrieves enforcement report narratives covering 1MDB from Enforcement Report 2022/2023, which details prohibition orders against former officers of BSI Bank and Goldman Sachs Singapore. MAS issued a lifetime prohibition order against Ng Chong Hwa (Roger Ng), a former Managing Director of Goldman Sachs (Singapore), after his conviction in the US for conspiracy to launder monies misappropriated from 1MDB. MAS also issued a 10-year prohibition order against Raj Sriram, former Deputy CEO of BSI Bank, for his neglect contributing to BSI's failure to file STRs on 1MDB-related transactions.

questions.query: How has MAS's approach to AI governance evolved from the FEAT principles to the current consultation, and how many regulatory instruments cover AI governance?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL for SELECT COUNT(*) FROM regulatory_instruments WHERE 'ai_governance' = ANY(topic_tags) returns 4 instruments. Vector search retrieves content showing the evolution: FEAT Principles (2018) as foundation, AI Model Risk Management paper (2024) with thematic review observations on good practices, MindForge Handbook (2026) operationalising and extending FEAT principles, and the AI Risk Management Consultation (2025) proposing formal guidelines.

questions.query: What enforcement actions has MAS taken against banks in the regulated entities table, and what outsourcing control requirements should these banks be following?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL join of enforcement_actions with regulated_entities for entity_type IN ('local_bank', 'full_bank') returns 5 actions: United Overseas Bank Limited (prohibition_order, 2025-07-04, aml_cft), JPMorgan Chase Bank N.A. (civil_penalty, 2024-12-02, business_conduct), DBS Bank Ltd (composition_penalty, 2023-06-21, aml_cft), Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation Limited (prohibition_order, 2022-02-24, fit_and_proper), United Overseas Bank Limited (prohibition_order, 2017-05-30, fit_and_proper). Vector search retrieves outsourcing guidelines for banks covering due diligence, cloud computing, and cross-border outsourcing requirements.

questions.query: What were the key enforcement trends in the 2022/2023 period according to structured data, and how does the enforcement report narrative describe these trends?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL aggregation for 2022-2023 shows 100 enforcement actions total. By violation category: fit_and_proper (48), other (22), market_abuse (21), aml_cft (7), business_conduct (2). By action type: prohibition_order (49), criminal_conviction (31), composition_penalty (9), civil_penalty (8), reprimand (3). Vector search retrieves Enforcement Report 2022/2023 narrative which covers Wirecard enforcement actions, $7.1M AML penalties, and overall enforcement patterns for the period.

questions.query: What enforcement actions has MAS taken against Citibank Singapore Limited?
questions.ground_truth_answer: 3 enforcement records match Citibank: (1) Citibank Singapore Limited — composition_penalty, aml_cft, 2025-07-04 (MAS regulatory actions against 9 FIs for AML breaches), (2) Citibank N.A., Singapore Branch — composition_penalty, aml_cft, 2023-06-21 (MAS penalises 3 banks and an insurer), (3) Citibank N.A., Singapore Branch — composition_penalty, aml_cft, 2025-07-04 (9 FIs for AML breaches).

questions.query: What about OCBC?
questions.ground_truth_answer: 2 enforcement records match OCBC: (1) OCBC Singapore — composition_penalty, aml_cft, 2023-06-21 (MAS penalises 3 banks and an insurer for AML breaches), (2) OCBC Securities Pte Ltd — criminal_conviction, other, 2022-12-19 (Kenneth Goh conviction for offences under Securities and Futures Act). The agent must resolve the coreference ('What about') to understand this means enforcement actions against OCBC.

questions.query: For both of those banks, what AML requirements under Notice 626 were they expected to follow?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Coreference to Citibank and OCBC from previous turns. Vector search for Notice 626 AML/CFT requirements for banks including CDD, ongoing monitoring, suspicious transaction reporting, and sanctions screening obligations.

questions.query: What are MAS's outsourcing requirements for banks?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The Outsourcing Guidelines (Banks) effective December 2023 cover due diligence, cloud computing, cross-border outsourcing requirements, ongoing oversight of service providers, and access arrangements for MAS.

questions.query: How many enforcement actions were for outsourcing violations?
questions.ground_truth_answer: 0 enforcement actions have violation_category = 'outsourcing'. The database contains no outsourcing-related enforcement actions. The actual violation categories in the database are: fit_and_proper (163), other (84), market_abuse (44), aml_cft (35), business_conduct (9), disclosure (2).

questions.query: What about recent industry developments in outsourcing regulation?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Web search for recent MAS outsourcing regulatory developments beyond the corpus. May return information about cloud-specific guidance, cross-border regulatory cooperation, or updated outsourcing expectations.

questions.query: Going back to the guidelines, what specifically do they say about cloud computing?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Coreference callback to the outsourcing guidelines from turn 1. The cloud computing annex (Annex 2) provides high-level guidance covering cloud service delivery models (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS), deployment types (private, public, hybrid), and key risks including data access, confidentiality, integrity, sovereignty, recoverability, regulatory compliance, and auditing. It emphasizes data segregation, robust access controls, and applying the same due diligence and risk management practices as for other outsourcing arrangements.

questions.query: Give me an overview of MAS's approach to AI governance.
questions.ground_truth_answer: Overview spanning FEAT principles (2018) as the foundation, AI Model Risk Management paper (2024) with thematic review findings, and MindForge Handbook (2026) operationalising FEAT. The AI Risk Management Consultation (2025) proposes formal guidelines.

questions.query: How many regulatory instruments specifically cover AI governance?
questions.ground_truth_answer: SQL query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM regulatory_instruments WHERE 'ai_governance' = ANY(topic_tags). Returns 4 instruments.

questions.query: What are the specific fairness and ethics requirements from the FEAT principles?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The FEAT fairness principles cover two aspects: justifiability (individuals or groups should not be systematically disadvantaged through AIDA-driven decisions unless justified, and use of personal attributes must be justified) and accuracy/bias (data and models should be regularly reviewed for accuracy and to minimize unintentional bias). The ethics principles require that use of AIDA is aligned with the firm's ethical standards, values and codes of conduct, and that AIDA-driven decisions are held to at least the same ethical standards as human-driven decisions.

questions.query: What are the most common types of enforcement actions MAS takes?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Prohibition orders are most common (166), followed by criminal convictions (64), composition penalties (50), civil penalties (25), reprimands (14), other (11), and licence revocations (7). Total 337.

questions.query: Tell me more about the market abuse cases specifically.
questions.ground_truth_answer: 44 enforcement records for violation_category = 'market_abuse', including stock market manipulation masterminds (Blumont Group, Asiasons, LionGold — 2013 penny stocks crash), insider trading cases, false trading convictions, and civil penalty actions for insider trading.

questions.query: What does MAS say about how it decides to pursue enforcement action?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Enforcement Monograph content on MAS enforcement strategy, decision framework, and the range of tools available for enforcement including civil penalties, criminal prosecution, prohibition orders, and other sanctions.

questions.query: Are there any recent enforcement cases not yet in your database?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Web search for MAS enforcement actions after the corpus cutoff. The agent should recognise this as a temporal gap query where the user is asking about information potentially newer than the database.

questions.query: Explain the key requirements of MAS TRM Guidelines for cyber security.
questions.ground_truth_answer: TRM Guidelines content on cyber security including surveillance, penetration testing, vulnerability management, security operations, and cyber threat intelligence.

questions.query: Now switch to BCM Guidelines — what are the requirements for disaster recovery?
questions.ground_truth_answer: The BCM Guidelines cover recovery requirements including Service Recovery Time Objectives (SRTOs) for critical business services and Recovery Time Objectives (RTOs) for critical business functions. Testing requirements (Section 7) include validating BCP effectiveness, crisis management exercises, stress testing under severe scenarios, and verifying SRTOs/RTOs can be met. The document takes a service-centric approach focused on critical business services. Note: Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) are covered in the TRM Guidelines (Section 8.2), not the BCM Guidelines, and IT disaster recovery plan testing is also in TRM (Section 8.3).

questions.query: Going back to TRM, how do its incident reporting requirements differ from BCM's incident management?
questions.ground_truth_answer: TRM focuses on IT incident management (Section 7.7): establishing a framework to restore affected IT services, including procedures for handling IT/cyber incidents, evidence preservation, and roles for recording, analysis, escalation, decision-making, resolution, and monitoring. Root cause analysis is covered under Problem Management (Section 7.8). BCM focuses on broader incident and crisis management (Section 9): managing incidents to resume critical business services within SRTOs/RTOs, crisis management structure with defined roles and triggers, stakeholder communication plans, and the requirement to notify MAS within one hour of discovering incidents where business operations will be severely disrupted (Section 9.6). The one-hour MAS notification requirement is in BCM, not TRM.

multi_turn_chains.description: Entity-focused enforcement chain with coreference resolution
Tool switching across turns: vector -> SQL -> web -> vector callback
Deepening AI governance inquiry across tools
Enforcement deep dive with tool transitions
Cross-guideline comparison with topic switch and callback
